2603.26233.title: Ask or Assume? Uncertainty-Aware Clarification-Seeking in Coding Agents
2603.26233.paper_summary: As Large Language Model (LLM) agents are increasingly deployed in open-ended domains like software engineering, they frequently encounter underspecified instructions that lack crucial context. While human developers naturally resolve underspecification by asking clarifying questions, current agents are largely optimized for autonomous execution. In this work, we systematically evaluate the clarification-seeking abilities of LLM agents on an underspecified variant of SWE-bench Verified. We propose an uncertainty-aware multi-agent scaffold that explicitly decouples underspecification detection from code execution. Our results demonstrate that this multi-agent system using OpenHands + Claude Sonnet 4.5 achieves a 69.40% task resolve rate, significantly outperforming a standard single-agent setup (61.20%) and closing the performance gap with agents operating on fully specified instructions. Furthermore, we find that the multi-agent system exhibits well-calibrated uncertainty, conserving queries on simple tasks while proactively seeking information on more complex issues. These findings indicate that current models can be turned into proactive collaborators, where agents independently recognize when to ask questions to elicit missing information in real-world, underspecified tasks.
2603.26233.paper_summary_zh: 隨著大型語言模型（LLM）代理在軟體工程等開放性領域的應用日益增多，它們經常會遇到缺乏關鍵上下文的未具體指示。雖然人類開發者自然會通過提出澄清問題來解決未具體化的情況，但目前的代理主要是針對自主執行進行優化。在這項工作中，我們系統性地評估了LLM代理在一個未具體化的SWE-bench Verified變體上的澄清尋求能力。我們提出了一個不確定性感知的多代理架構，明確將未具體化檢測與代碼執行解耦。我們的結果顯示，這個使用OpenHands + Claude Sonnet 4.5的多代理系統達到了69.40%的任務解決率，顯著超過了標準的單代理設置（61.20%），並縮小了與在完全具體化指示上運行的代理之間的性能差距。此外，我們發現這個多代理系統表現出良好的不確定性校準，在簡單任務上節省查詢，同時在更複雜的問題上主動尋求信息。這些發現表明，當前的模型可以轉變為主動的合作夥伴，代理能夠獨立識別何時提出問題以引出現實世界中未具體化任務中的缺失信息。
2603.26233.author: Nicholas Edwards et.al.
